title: Harvey Cedars, New Jersey

infobox.value: 488th of 565 in state 23rd of 33 in county[[1]](#cite_note-CensusArea-1)

is a coastal [borough](/wiki/Borough_(New_Jersey) "Borough (New Jersey)") situated on the [Jersey Shore](/wiki/Jersey_Shore "Jersey Shore"), in southern [Ocean County](/wiki/Ocean_County,_New_Jersey "Ocean County, New Jersey"), in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey"). The borough encompasses a narrow strip of [Long Beach Island](/wiki/Long_Beach_Island "Long Beach Island") fronting both the [Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean") and [Barnegat Bay](/wiki/Barnegat_Bay "Barnegat Bay"). As of the [2020 United States census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), the borough's population was 391,[[9]](#cite_note-LWD2020-9) its highest [decennial count](/wiki/United_States_census "United States census") ever and an increase of 54 (+16.0%) from the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census") count of 337,[[18]](#cite_note-Census2010-18)[[19]](#cite_note-LWD2010-19) which in turn reflected a decline of 22 (−6.1%) from the 359 counted in the [2000 census](/wiki/2000_United_States_census "2000 United States census").[[20]](#cite_note-20)

**Harvey Cedars** is a coastal [borough](/wiki/Borough_(New_Jersey) "Borough (New Jersey)") situated on the [Jersey Shore](/wiki/Jersey_Shore "Jersey Shore"), in southern [Ocean County](/wiki/Ocean_County,_New_Jersey "Ocean County, New Jersey"), in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey"). The borough encompasses a narrow strip of [Long Beach Island](/wiki/Long_Beach_Island "Long Beach Island") fronting both the [Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean") and [Barnegat Bay](/wiki/Barnegat_Bay "Barnegat Bay"). As of the [2020 United States census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), the borough's population was 391,[[9]](#cite_note-LWD2020-9) its highest [decennial count](/wiki/United_States_census "United States census") ever and an increase of 54 (+16.0%) from the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census") count of 337,[[18]](#cite_note-Census2010-18)[[19]](#cite_note-LWD2010-19) which in turn reflected a decline of 22 (−6.1%) from the 359 counted in the [2000 census](/wiki/2000_United_States_census "2000 United States census").[[20]](#cite_note-20)

Harvey Cedars was incorporated as a borough by an act of the [New Jersey Legislature](/wiki/New_Jersey_Legislature "New Jersey Legislature") on December 15, 1894, from portions of Union Township (now [Barnegat Township](/wiki/Barnegat_Township,_New_Jersey "Barnegat Township, New Jersey")), based on the results of a [referendum](/wiki/Referendum "Referendum") held on November 20, 1894.[[21]](#cite_note-Story-21) The community's post office was initially known as High Point, the name of a neighborhood in the borough that lies at a higher elevation, before a name change was made in the 1930s at the request of the United States Postal Service to differentiate the post office from the northern New Jersey community of High Point in [Sussex County](/wiki/Sussex_County,_New_Jersey "Sussex County, New Jersey").[[22]](#cite_note-22)

The majority of the housing units in the borough are seasonal houses used primarily in the summer by owners who live elsewhere, bringing the summer population to 12,000.[[23]](#cite_note-23) The borough's quiet character and bay and ocean access make housing very expensive, with many bay or oceanfront houses priced at $2 million or more. Despite the borough's small size, its property was assessed at over $1.28 billion in 2019.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Before [Long Beach Island](/wiki/Long_Beach_Island "Long Beach Island") was developed, its northern area, from the [Barnegat Inlet](/wiki/Barnegat_Inlet "Barnegat Inlet") to the Great Swamp (now [Surf City](/wiki/Surf_City,_New_Jersey "Surf City, New Jersey")), was covered with Atlantic white cedar ([chamaecyparis thyoides](/wiki/Chamaecyparis_thyoides "Chamaecyparis thyoides")). Early inhabitants of the area harvested salt hay ([spartina patens](/wiki/Spartina_patens "Spartina patens")) and seaweed to make a living.

The earliest reference to the area was a deed from 1751 that called the place "Harvest Quarters".[[25]](#cite_note-25) The name "Harvey Cedars" may be derived from the "harvest" housing used by these farmers and the "cedars" that grew in the area.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the borough had a total area of 1.19 square miles (3.08 km2), including 0.56 square miles (1.45 km2) of land and 0.63 square miles (1.62 km2) of water (52.61%).[[1]](#cite_note-CensusArea-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GR1-2)

[Unincorporated communities](/wiki/Local_government_in_New_Jersey#Unincorporated_communities "Local government in New Jersey"), localities and place names located partially or completely within the borough include High Point.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

The borough borders the Ocean County municipalities of [Barnegat Township](/wiki/Barnegat_Township,_New_Jersey "Barnegat Township, New Jersey"), [Long Beach Township](/wiki/Long_Beach_Township,_New_Jersey "Long Beach Township, New Jersey") and [Stafford Township](/wiki/Stafford_Township,_New_Jersey "Stafford Township, New Jersey").[[28]](#cite_note-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30)

### 2010 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2010 census")]

The [2010 United States census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census") counted 337 people, 169 households, and 110 families in the borough. The population density was 604.6 inhabitants per square mile (233.4/km2). There were 1,214 housing units at an average density of 2,178.0 per square mile (840.9/km2). The racial makeup was 99.11% (334) [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._census) "White (U.S. census)"), 0.59% (2) [Black or African American](/wiki/Black_(U.S._census) "Black (U.S. census)"), 0.00% (0) [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._census) "Native American (U.S. census)"), 0.30% (1) [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._census) "Asian (U.S. census)"), 0.00% (0) [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. census)"), 0.00% (0) from [other races](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_census "Race and ethnicity in the United States census"), and 0.00% (0) from two or more races. [Hispanic or Latino](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._census) "Hispanic (U.S. census)") of any race were 0.89% (3) of the population.[[18]](#cite_note-Census2010-18)

Of the 169 households, 13.6% had children under the age of 18; 59.8% were married couples living together; 5.3% had a female householder with no husband present and 34.9% were non-families. Of all households, 32.0% were made up of individuals and 18.3% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 1.99 and the average family size was 2.47.[[18]](#cite_note-Census2010-18)

11.6% of the population were under the age of 18, 4.5% from 18 to 24, 10.1% from 25 to 44, 32.0% from 45 to 64, and 41.8% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 61.6 years. For every 100 females, the population had 97.1 males. For every 100 females ages 18 and older there were 97.4 males.[[18]](#cite_note-Census2010-18)

The Census Bureau's 2006–2010 [American Community Survey](/wiki/American_Community_Survey "American Community Survey") showed that (in 2010 [inflation-adjusted](/wiki/Inflation_adjustment "Inflation adjustment") dollars) [median household income](/wiki/Median_household_income "Median household income") was $106,875 (with a margin of error of +/− $15,693) and the median family income was $112,656 (+/− $8,889). Males had a median income of $85,625 (+/− $32,732) versus $51,875 (+/− $42,840) for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the borough was $74,525 (+/− $13,683). About 3.0% of families and 4.2% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including none of those under age 18 and 6.2% of those age 65 or over.[[38]](#cite_note-38)

There were 167 households, out of which 16.8% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 61.7% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 4.2% had a female householder with no husband present, and 32.9% were non-families. 29.3% of all households were made up of individuals, and 15.6% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. In the borough the population was spread out, with 14.5% under the age of 18, 4.2% from 18 to 24, 22.0% from 25 to 44, 29.0% from 45 to 64, and 30.4% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 54 years. For every 100 females, there were 102.8 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 106.0 males.[[36]](#cite_note-Census2000-36)[[37]](#cite_note-Census2000SF1-37)

The median income for a household in the borough was $61,875, and the median income for a family was $69,722. Males had a median income of $71,042 versus $32,361 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the borough was $36,757. About 2.6% of families and 5.1% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 5.1% of those under age 18 and 2.9% of those age 65 or over.[[36]](#cite_note-Census2000-36)[[37]](#cite_note-Census2000SF1-37)

## Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Government")]

### Local government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Local government")]

The Borough of Harvey Cedars has operated under the [Walsh Act](/wiki/Walsh_Act "Walsh Act") form of New Jersey municipal government since 1923.[[39]](#cite_note-39)[[40]](#cite_note-40) The borough is one of 30 municipalities (of the 564) statewide that use the [commission form of government](/wiki/City_commission_government "City commission government").[[41]](#cite_note-41) The governing body is comprised of the three-member Board of Commissioners whose members are elected [at-large](/wiki/At-large "At-large") on a [non-partisan](/wiki/Non-partisan_democracy "Non-partisan democracy") basis to serve concurrent four-year terms of office in voting held as part of the November general election.[[6]](#cite_note-DataBook-6) Each commissioner is assigned to oversee and administer a department. The Mayor and Deputy Mayor are elected by the Board from among its members. The mayor has no veto power.

As of 2024[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit), members of the Harvey Cedars Board of Commissioners are
[Mayor](/wiki/Mayor "Mayor") Johnathan M. Imperiale (Commissioner of Public Affairs and Public Safety),
Joseph F. Gieger (Commissioner of Public Works, Parks and Public Property) and
Paul George Rice (Commissioner of Revenue and Finance), all of whom are serving concurrent four-year terms of office that expire on December 31, 2027.[[3]](#cite_note-Government-3)[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-OceanProfile-43)[[44]](#cite_note-OceanOfficials-44)[[45]](#cite_note-Ocean2023-45)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Federal, state and county representation")]

Harvey Cedars is located in the 2nd Congressional District[[46]](#cite_note-PCR2012-46) and is part of New Jersey's 9th state legislative district.[[47]](#cite_note-Districts2011-47)[[48]](#cite_note-LWV2019-48)[[49]](#cite_note-49) Prior to the 2010 Census, Harvey Cedars had been part of the [3rd Congressional District](/wiki/New_Jersey%27s_3rd_congressional_district "New Jersey's 3rd congressional district"), a change made by the [New Jersey Redistricting Commission](/wiki/New_Jersey_Redistricting_Commission "New Jersey Redistricting Commission") that took effect in January 2013, based on the results of the November 2012 general elections.[[50]](#cite_note-LWV2011-50)

For the [119th United States Congress](/wiki/119th_United_States_Congress "119th United States Congress"), [New Jersey's 2nd congressional district](/wiki/New_Jersey%27s_2nd_congressional_district "New Jersey's 2nd congressional district") is represented by [Jeff Van Drew](/wiki/Jeff_Van_Drew "Jeff Van Drew") ([R](/wiki/Republican_Party_(United_States) "Republican Party (United States)"), [Dennis Township](/wiki/Dennis_Township,_New_Jersey "Dennis Township, New Jersey")).[[51]](#cite_note-51) New Jersey is represented in the [United States Senate](/wiki/United_States_Senate "United States Senate") by [Democrats](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)") [Cory Booker](/wiki/Cory_Booker "Cory Booker") ([Newark](/wiki/Newark,_New_Jersey "Newark, New Jersey"), term ends 2027) and [Andy Kim](/wiki/Andy_Kim "Andy Kim") ([Moorestown](/wiki/Moorestown,_New_Jersey "Moorestown, New Jersey"), term ends 2031).[[52]](#cite_note-52)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Education")]

For [pre-kindergarten](/wiki/Pre-kindergarten "Pre-kindergarten") through [sixth grade](/wiki/Sixth_grade "Sixth grade"), public school students attend the [Long Beach Island Consolidated School District](/wiki/Long_Beach_Island_Consolidated_School_District "Long Beach Island Consolidated School District"), which also serves students from [Barnegat Light](/wiki/Barnegat_Light,_New_Jersey "Barnegat Light, New Jersey"), [Long Beach Township](/wiki/Long_Beach_Township,_New_Jersey "Long Beach Township, New Jersey"), [Ship Bottom](/wiki/Ship_Bottom,_New_Jersey "Ship Bottom, New Jersey") and [Surf City](/wiki/Surf_City,_New_Jersey "Surf City, New Jersey").[[6]](#cite_note-DataBook-6)[[86]](#cite_note-86)[[87]](#cite_note-87) As of the 2020–21 school year, the district, comprised of two schools, had an enrollment of 215 students and 30.7 classroom teachers (on an [FTE](/wiki/Full-time_equivalent "Full-time equivalent") basis), for a [student–teacher ratio](/wiki/Student%E2%80%93teacher_ratio "Student–teacher ratio") of 7.0:1.[[88]](#cite_note-NCES-88) Schools in the district (with 2020–21 enrollment data from the [National Center for Education Statistics](/wiki/National_Center_for_Education_Statistics "National Center for Education Statistics")[[89]](#cite_note-89)) are
Ethel Jacobsen School[[90]](#cite_note-90) in Surf City with 111 students in pre-kindergarten to second grade and
Long Beach Island Grade School[[91]](#cite_note-91) in Ship Bottom with 125 students in grades 3–6.[[92]](#cite_note-92)[[93]](#cite_note-93)[[94]](#cite_note-94) The district's board of education is comprised of nine members who are directly elected from the constituent municipalities on a staggered basis, with three members elected each year.[[95]](#cite_note-95)[[96]](#cite_note-96) Of the nine seats, one is elected from Harvey Cedars.[[97]](#cite_note-97)

Students in public school for [seventh](/wiki/Seventh_grade "Seventh grade") through [twelfth grades](/wiki/Twelfth_grade "Twelfth grade") attend the [Southern Regional School District](/wiki/Southern_Regional_School_District "Southern Regional School District"), which serves the five municipalities in the Long Beach Island Consolidated School District, along with students from [Beach Haven](/wiki/Beach_Haven,_New_Jersey "Beach Haven, New Jersey") and [Stafford Township](/wiki/Stafford_Township,_New_Jersey "Stafford Township, New Jersey"), as well as students from [Ocean Township](/wiki/Ocean_Township,_Ocean_County,_New_Jersey "Ocean Township, Ocean County, New Jersey") (including its [Waretown](/wiki/Waretown,_New_Jersey "Waretown, New Jersey") section) who attend as part of a [sending/receiving relationship](/wiki/Sending/receiving_relationship "Sending/receiving relationship").[[6]](#cite_note-DataBook-6)[[98]](#cite_note-98)[[99]](#cite_note-99) Schools in the district (with 2020–21 enrollment data from the [National Center for Education Statistics](/wiki/National_Center_for_Education_Statistics "National Center for Education Statistics")[[100]](#cite_note-100)) are
Southern Regional Middle School[[101]](#cite_note-101) with 902 students in grades 7–8 and
[Southern Regional High School](/wiki/Southern_Regional_High_School "Southern Regional High School")[[102]](#cite_note-102) with 1,975 students in grades 9–12.[[103]](#cite_note-103)[[104]](#cite_note-104) Both schools are in the [Manahawkin](/wiki/Manahawkin,_New_Jersey "Manahawkin, New Jersey") section of Stafford Township.

At the time of its founding in 1957, the Southern Regional School District had a roughly equal number of students from Long Beach Island and Stafford Township.
By 2016, the overwhelming majority of students were from Stafford Township, accounting for nearly 90% of enrollment. These demographic changes have led to significant discrepancies in the cost per pupil sent to the district from each community, with Harvey Cedars and Long Beach Township paying more than $200,000 per pupil, while Stafford Township's costs are $3,600 for each student. These widely different costs result from a formula that uses the taxable property value in each municipality to apportion costs, which means that municipalities with relatively high property values and small numbers of students pay a higher share of total district costs. Some residents of Long Beach Island communities are seeking to amend the formula to take advantage of a 1993 law that allows districts to use both property value and enrollment to allocate property taxes, though that would require passage of referendums in each municipality.[[105]](#cite_note-105)

### Roads and highways

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Roads and highways")]

As of May 2010[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit), the borough had a total of 9.82 miles (15.80 km) of roadways, of which 7.59 miles (12.21 km) were maintained by the municipality and 2.23 miles (3.59 km) by Ocean County.[[106]](#cite_note-106)

No Interstate, U.S. or state highways serve Harvey Cedars. The main road serving the borough is [County Route 607](/wiki/County_Route_607_(Ocean_County,_New_Jersey) "County Route 607 (Ocean County, New Jersey)") (Long Beach Boulevard).

### Public transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Public transportation")]

Ocean County Ride provides bus service on the OC9 LBI North route between [Barnegat Light](/wiki/Barnegat_Light,_New_Jersey "Barnegat Light, New Jersey") and [Manahawkin](/wiki/Manahawkin,_New_Jersey "Manahawkin, New Jersey") / [Stafford Township](/wiki/Stafford_Township,_New_Jersey "Stafford Township, New Jersey").[[107]](#cite_note-107)

The LBI Shuttle operates along Long Beach Boulevard, providing free service every 5 to 20 minutes from 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM. It serves the Long Beach Island municipalities / communities of Barnegat Light, [Loveladies](/wiki/Loveladies,_New_Jersey "Loveladies, New Jersey"), Harvey Cedars, [North Beach](/wiki/North_Beach,_New_Jersey "North Beach, New Jersey"), [Surf City](/wiki/Surf_City,_New_Jersey "Surf City, New Jersey"), [Ship Bottom](/wiki/Ship_Bottom,_New_Jersey "Ship Bottom, New Jersey"), [Long Beach Township](/wiki/Long_Beach_Township,_New_Jersey "Long Beach Township, New Jersey"), [Beach Haven](/wiki/Beach_Haven,_New_Jersey "Beach Haven, New Jersey") and [Holgate](/wiki/Holgate,_New_Jersey "Holgate, New Jersey").[[108]](#cite_note-108)

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Climate")]

According to the [Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification") system, Harvey Cedars has a [humid subtropical climate](/wiki/Humid_subtropical_climate "Humid subtropical climate") (*Cfa*) with hot, moderately humid summers, cool winters, and year-around precipitation. Cfa climates are characterized by all months having an average mean temperature above 32.0 °F (0.0 °C), at least four months with an average mean temperature at or above 50.0 °F (10.0 °C), at least one month with an average mean temperature at or above 71.6 °F (22.0 °C) and no significant precipitation difference between seasons. During the summer months in Harvey Cedars, a cooling afternoon [sea breeze](/wiki/Sea_breeze "Sea breeze") is present on most days, but episodes of extreme heat and humidity can occur with [heat index](/wiki/Heat_index "Heat index") values at or above 95.0 °F (35.0 °C). During the winter months, episodes of extreme cold and wind can occur with [wind chill](/wiki/Wind_chill "Wind chill") values below 0.0 °F (−17.8 °C). The [plant hardiness zone](/wiki/Plant_hardiness_zone "Plant hardiness zone") at Harvey Cedars Beach is 7a with an average annual extreme minimum air temperature of 3.5 °F (−15.8 °C).[[109]](#cite_note-USDA-109) The average seasonal (November–April) snowfall total is 12 to 18 inches (30 to 46 cm) and the average snowiest month is February which corresponds with the annual peak in [nor'easter](/wiki/Nor%27easter "Nor'easter") activity.

## Ecology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harvey_Cedars,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Ecology")]

According to the [A. W. Kuchler](/wiki/A._W._Kuchler "A. W. Kuchler") U.S. [potential natural vegetation](/wiki/Potential_natural_vegetation "Potential natural vegetation") types, Harvey Cedars would have a dominant vegetation type of Northern [Cordgrass](/wiki/Cordgrass "Cordgrass") (*73*) with a dominant vegetation form of [Coastal](/wiki/Coastal "Coastal") [Prairie](/wiki/Prairie "Prairie") (*20*).[[112]](#cite_note-Conservation_Biology_Institute-112)

* [Francis Biddle](/wiki/Francis_Biddle "Francis Biddle") (1886–1968), [United States Attorney General](/wiki/United_States_Attorney_General "United States Attorney General") from 1941 to 1945, owned a summer home in Harvey Cedars.[[113]](#cite_note-113)[[114]](#cite_note-114)
* [Wendy Mae Chambers](/wiki/Wendy_Mae_Chambers "Wendy Mae Chambers") (born 1953), composer[[115]](#cite_note-115)
* [Paul L. Gill](/wiki/Paul_L._Gill "Paul L. Gill") (1894–1938), watercolor painter and teacher[[116]](#cite_note-Gills-116)
* [Sue May Gill](/wiki/Sue_May_Gill "Sue May Gill") (1887–1989), artist and member of the [Philadelphia Ten](/wiki/Philadelphia_Ten "Philadelphia Ten")[[116]](#cite_note-Gills-116)
* [Leon Kelly](/wiki/Leon_Kelly "Leon Kelly") (1901–1982), artist best known for his contributions to [Surrealism](/wiki/Surrealism "Surrealism")[[117]](#cite_note-117)
* [Nathan Gregory Silvermaster](/wiki/Nathan_Gregory_Silvermaster "Nathan Gregory Silvermaster") (1898–1964), economist with the United States [War Production Board](/wiki/War_Production_Board "War Production Board") during World War II, who was the head of a large Communist spy ring in the U.S. government[[118]](#cite_note-118)
